runs.text: The scorecard orders diligence, not expected wins.
The table points to where to look first; the decision still depends on fit, qualification path, incumbent lock-up, and whether work can move to a new site.

runs.text: Modular leads because it best matches the entry thesis.
The modular cut is only $381M, but it is most aligned with distributed build; broad component manufacturing remains the sizing envelope to triage by bucket.

runs.text: Sources: (1) modeled scenario SAM, FY2022–FY2027; (2) FFATA/FSRS supplier-visibility evidence and qualification-burden assessment; (3) DDG and submarine program implications analysis
